task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent opening or printing the contents of ~/es_translations.json (e.g., using cat, less, or a text editor).
Screenshots show the agent creating or editing a PHP file within /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/resources/lang/es/ that returns an array of the Spanish translations.

task_completion.description: Update login views to use translation functions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent editing a Blade template file (e.g., login.blade.php) in /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/resources/views/ to replace English text with Laravel translation directives like {{ __('...') }} or @lang('...').

task_completion.description: Configure the application's default locale to Spanish
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent editing the application configuration (such as config/app.php or .env in the Socioboard directory) to set the locale to 'es'.

task_completion.description: Verify the translated login page in a browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser open to http://localhost/login where the UI text displays the Spanish translations: 'Correo electrónico', 'Contraseña', 'Recuérdame', and 'Iniciar sesión'.

integrity.description: Ensure translations are not hardcoded in the views
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent used Laravel's translation functions in the Blade views instead of typing the Spanish strings directly into the HTML.

integrity.description: Ensure a real web browser is used for verification
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the final verification of the login page is done in a graphical web browser, not just by fetching the HTML source code via curl or similar tools.

integrity.description: Ensure the correct application directory is modified
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent modified the files in the actual /opt/socioboard/socioboard-web-php/ directory, not a copy or a different path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The translation glossary is located at /home/ga/es_translations.json. The expected Spanish strings on the login page are exactly: 'Correo electrónico', 'Contraseña', 'Recuérdame', and 'Iniciar sesión'.
